events.title: BP-reported pipeline release and oil sheen in Mississippi Canyon Block 109 (Gulf of Mexico)
events.description: A possible offshore pipeline release was reported to the U.S. Coast Guard by British Petroleum. An overflight observed a large sheen with heavy streaking traced to a crack in an 8-inch pipeline, and dispersants and response resources were deployed as currents were thought to keep oil offshore.

location.name: Gulf of Mexico — Mississippi Canyon Block 109 (off Louisiana)

sources.source_name: National Response Center (Texas A&M NRC archive)
sources.headline: Incident report (NRC)

key_quotes.quote: USCG received a report from British Petroleum of a possible pipeline release.
Overflight observed a large sheen with heavy streaking... traced to a crack in an 8-inch pipeline.

events.title: Crude oil spill from Koch Pipeline receiving station into Marcelinas Creek (Karnes County, Texas)
events.description: A crude oil release from a Koch Pipeline receiving station near Falls City, Texas reportedly spilled more than 30,000 gallons. Floodwaters carried oil downstream, coating vegetation and creek banks along Marcelinas Creek, a tributary of the San Antonio River.

location.name: Near Falls City, Karnes County, Texas — Marcelinas Creek

sources.headline: I Love the 90s: Texas Is Poised to Resolve Long-Ago Oil Spill

key_quotes.quote: a release of crude oil... spilled more than 30,000 gallons

events.title: EPA enforcement action over nitrogen dioxide release at Philip Services facility (Tacoma Tideflats, Washington)
events.description: The U.S. EPA announced an administrative complaint seeking civil penalties related to a nitrogen dioxide release during a tank-truck transfer at a hazardous-waste facility. EPA said the release produced a tall orange plume and that required notifications to authorities were not made promptly.

location.name: Tacoma Tideflats, Tacoma, Washington, USA (1701 Alexander Ave.)

sources.headline: EPA announces complaint seeking penalties after nitrogen dioxide release at Tacoma facility

key_quotes.quote: EPA announced an administrative complaint seeking civil penalties after a nitrogen dioxide release
produced a tall orange plume and prompted evacuations and roadblocks

events.title: South-central Texas floods in the Guadalupe & San Antonio River Basins
events.description: A major storm produced severe flooding across south-central Texas, particularly in the Guadalupe and San Antonio River Basins, during October 17–18, 1998. The event impacted the San Antonio–Austin corridor and surrounding areas.

location.name: South-central Texas, USA (Guadalupe and San Antonio River Basins; San Antonio–Austin corridor)

sources.headline: Service Assessment: South and Central Texas Floods of October 1998

key_quotes.quote: severe flooding across south-central Texas... during October 17–18, 1998
South and Central Texas Floods of October 1998

events.title: Widespread flooding across south & southeast Texas (Oct 17–22, 1998)
events.description: Torrential rains around October 17–18, 1998 contributed to widespread and deadly flooding across south and southeast Texas. NOAA produced a service assessment covering the October 17–22 event period.

sources.headline: Service Assessment: South and Central Texas Floods of October 1998

key_quotes.quote: Service Assessment: South and Central Texas Floods of October 1998

events.title: Hurricane Mitch forms and drives extreme flooding impacts in Central America
events.description: Hurricane Mitch formed on October 22, 1998, intensified to Category 5 by October 26, and produced extreme rainfall leading to severe flooding impacts in Central America through late October. Honduras and Nicaragua were among the most affected countries.

sources.headline: 25 years later: Looking back at the October monster named Mitch
Hurricane Mitch: Peak discharge for selected river reaches in Honduras

key_quotes.quote: Hurricane Mitch formed October 22... intensified to Category 5 by October 26

dedupe.cluster_hint: Hurricane Mitch Oct 1998 Central America flooding

events.title: Super Typhoon Zeb (Iliang) landfalls in northern Luzon
events.description: Super Typhoon Zeb (Iliang) made landfall in Luzon during October 14–15, 1998, bringing severe typhoon conditions to parts of the Philippines. Northern Luzon, including Isabela, was among the areas cited as impacted.

key_quotes.quote: Super Typhoon Zeb

events.title: Super Typhoon Babs (Loleng) impacts in the Philippines (late Oct 1998)
events.description: Super Typhoon Babs (Loleng) affected the Philippines in late October 1998, with landfalls reported during October 21–23 and associated rainfall and wind impacts. The Digital Typhoon entry is cited as a source summary for the storm.

key_quotes.quote: Digital Typhoon

events.title: Ticuantepe fault earthquake and swarm activity near Managua, Nicaragua
events.description: Earthquakes on or near the Ticuantepe fault were strongly felt in Ticuantepe and Managua. The sequence caused minor damage and initiated a swarm lasting about 18 days.

key_quotes.quote: ...Ticuantepe y Managua... daños menores... inició un enjambre... alrededor de 18 días.

events.title: Mount Edna brush fire near Banning threatens homes; air tanker crash kills pilot
events.description: A fast-moving brush fire near Banning in Riverside County burned thousands of acres and threatened homes, prompting evacuations. An air tanker assisting suppression efforts crashed, killing the pilot.

location.name: Near Banning, Riverside County, California, USA

key_quotes.quote: Fast-moving brush fire near Banning burned more than 5,000 acres and threatened homes
an air tanker crashed, killing the pilot

events.title: Taylor Fire burns between Beaumont and Calimesa during Santa Ana wind-driven outbreak
events.description: A separate wildfire between Beaumont and Calimesa in Riverside County burned thousands of acres during a broader Santa Ana wind-driven fire outbreak. The incident was reported alongside the larger Banning-area blaze.

location.name: Between Beaumont and Calimesa, Riverside County, California, USA

key_quotes.quote: burned thousands of acres between Beaumont and Calimesa

events.title: Rancho Bernardo brush fire destroys homes under construction
events.description: A brush fire in the Rancho Bernardo area of San Diego damaged or destroyed multiple homes under construction and threatened nearby occupied homes during Santa Ana wind conditions.

location.name: Rancho Bernardo, San Diego, California, USA

key_quotes.quote: brush fire in San Diego's Rancho Bernardo area destroyed multiple homes under construction

events.title: Tierrasanta brush fire prompts evacuations in San Diego
events.description: A wildfire threatened homes in the Tierrasanta area of San Diego and prompted evacuations during Santa Ana wind-driven fire conditions.

key_quotes.quote: threatened homes in Tierrasanta, prompting evacuations

events.title: UPI roundup: Riverside County brush fires destroy homes; deaths reported
events.description: Follow-up reporting summarized impacts from Riverside County brush fires, including destroyed homes, injuries, and deaths involving an air tanker pilot and a firefighter. The report described ongoing consequences from the fires burning in the county.

key_quotes.quote: destroy 13 homes
two deaths (air tanker pilot and a firefighter)

events.title: Red tide along South Texas Gulf Coast reported with fish kills and beach impacts
events.description: An archived red tide note describes a harmful algal bloom event on the South Texas Gulf Coast associated with fish kills and degraded beach conditions. Reports also raised concerns about impacts to shellfish harvesting.

location.name: South Padre Island area, Texas, USA

key_quotes.quote: Reports described an active red tide along South Texas beaches with fish carcasses on shore

events.title: Texas red tide status report notes dead fish and human irritation near Corpus Christi
events.description: A Texas harmful algal bloom archive entry documents red tide detections near the Corpus Christi area with dead fish reported on nearby Gulf beaches. The entry also notes reports of moderate respiratory or eye irritation among beachgoers.

key_quotes.quote: archived status report documents red tide detections and associated dead fish... near Corpus Christi

events.title: Archived EPA highlights page notes NOAA statement on record 1998 tropical coral bleaching
events.description: An archived EPA coral and reef highlights page notes that NOAA announced on October 15, 1998 that record-breaking amounts of coral bleaching occurred during 1998 in the tropics. The note indicates widespread stress on coral reef ecosystems during that year.

key_quotes.quote: NOAA’s October 15, 1998 announcement that record-breaking coral bleaching occurred during 1998 in the tropics

events.title: First U.S. Coral Reef Task Force meeting held at Biscayne National Park
events.description: An archived EPA page describes the first U.S. Coral Reef Task Force meeting held October 19–20, 1998 at Biscayne National Park. The meeting identified major reef threats and included resolutions acknowledging extensive 1998 coral bleaching and mortality.

key_quotes.quote: first-ever U.S. Coral Reef Task Force meeting was held October 19–20, 1998

events.title: EPA final rule on emissions standards for nonroad diesel engines
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency published a final rule setting emission standards for diesel engines used in nonroad equipment and some marine applications. The rule appears in the Federal Register dated October 23, 1998.

sources.headline: Final Rule: Control of Emissions of Air Pollution From Nonroad Diesel Engines and Fuel
Control of Emissions of Air Pollution From Nonroad Diesel Engines and Fuel (Final Rule)

key_quotes.quote: final rule setting emission standards for new nonroad compression-ignition (diesel) engines

dedupe.cluster_hint: US EPA final rule Federal Register 1998-10

events.title: EPA final State Implementation Rule for Subtitle D state permit program adequacy determinations
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency published a final rule establishing procedures for determining the adequacy of state permit programs for facilities regulated under RCRA Subtitle D. The rule was published in the Federal Register on October 23, 1998.

sources.headline: Subtitle D Regulated Facilities—State Permit Program Determination of Adequacy; State Implementation Rule

key_quotes.quote: State Permit Program Determination of Adequacy; State Implementation Rule

dedupe.cluster_hint: US EPA RCRA Subtitle D State Implementation Rule 1998-10-23

events.title: EPA final rule allowing alternatives to RCRA post-closure permits and added corrective-action flexibility
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency published a final rule revising RCRA regulations to allow alternative enforceable mechanisms in lieu of post-closure permits for certain hazardous waste units. The rule also provided flexibility related to corrective-action/remediation requirements and was published in the Federal Register on October 22, 1998.

sources.headline: Hazardous Waste Management System; Permit Modification; Hazardous Waste Management Permit Program; Removal of the Land Disposal Restrictions for Certain Wastes (post-closure permit alternatives rule)

key_quotes.quote: allow alternative enforceable mechanisms in lieu of post-closure permits

dedupe.cluster_hint: US EPA RCRA post-closure permit alternatives 1998-10-22

events.title: EPA final rule exempting alder bark from pesticide tolerance requirements as an inert ingredient
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency published a final rule establishing an exemption from the requirement of a tolerance for residues of alder bark when used as an inert ingredient in pesticide formulations applied to growing crops. The rule was published in the Federal Register on October 5, 1998.

sources.headline: Alder Bark; Exemption From the Requirement of a Tolerance

key_quotes.quote: Alder Bark; Exemption From the Requirement of a Tolerance

dedupe.cluster_hint: US EPA alder bark tolerance exemption 1998-10-05
